docs.title: A Full Regtest Setup Available on Command
docs.text:
This project is useful for software developers who need a simple and powerful regtest setup ready to go at will. Once powered up, the podman container offers you an Electrum server to connect to, a block explorer, a compact block filter enabled node, and awesome aliases to get stuff done:

just sendto <address>

just mine 21

just bitcoindlogs

just esploralogs

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
First, make sure you have Podman installed on your computer. Also useful (but not required) is the just cli tool.

Second, create a directory called podman somewhere on your machine and clone the repo:

mkdir ~/podman/
git clone https://github.com/thunderbiscuit/regtest-in-a-pod.git

Get into the container's directory and build the container:

cd regtest-in-a-pod/
podman machine start regtest
podman --connection regtest build --tag localhost/regtest:v1.0.0 --file ./Containerfile
podman create --name RegtestBitcoinEnv --publish 18443:18443 --publish 18444:18444 --publish 3002:3002 --publish 3003:3003 --publish 60401:60401 localhost/regtest:v1.1.0

You're now ready to start using the container! See the Usage page for all the details.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
Start the podman machine and RegtestBitcoinEnv container to fire up all services:

cd ~/podman/regtest-in-a-pod/
just start

# Alternatively, use
podman machine start regtest
podman start RegtestBitcoinEnv

From there you have access to a range of tools, all described below.

docs.title: 1. Bitcoin Core Full Node
docs.text:
# Mine 1 block
just mine

# Mine 10 blocks
just mine 10

# send 50 coins to specified address
just sendto <address>

To make full use of the node, load up the shell aliases contained in the aliases.sh file:

source aliases.sh

From there you can use the bitcoin-cli tool directly using the podcli alias:

podcli getblockchaininfo

docs.title: 2. Block Explorer
docs.text: You'll find a simple block explorer (Fast Bitcoin Block Explorer) at http://127.0.0.1:3003 in your browser.
